data.title: CGI inks multi-year AWS deal to drive AI adoption across U.S. public sector
data.description:
• CGI (GIB) said that it has entered a multi-year strategic collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services to accelerate AI adoption and streamline digital transformation across the U.S. public sector.
  • As a part of the deal, CGI (GIB) will deliver scalable, mission-critical cloud and AI solutions targeting government, education, healthcare and nonprofit organizations, addressing complex operational challenges.
  • The pact builds on CGI’s (GIB) existing AWS Premier Tier Services Partner status and its network of more than 150 technology alliances, reinforcing its role as a high‑end system‑integrator for AI‑integrated cloud solutions.
  • "Government agencies need AI that works at mission speed," said David Levy, Vice President, Worldwide Public Sector, AWS. "This collaboration puts advanced cloud and AI capabilities directly into the hands of agencies tackling their toughest challenges, from stopping fraud to securing critical systems to delivering better citizen services."

data.title: McDonald's tries to reclaim its value crown with under-$3 menu items, $4 breakfast deal
data.description:
McDonald's (MCD) is preparing to add a new round of value offerings to its McValue menu in the U.S. as it looks to win back budget-conscious customers. Starting in April, the chain plans to roll out a menu of items priced at $3 or less, alongside a $4 breakfast meal deal and lunch and dinner meal deals starting at $5.

The lower-priced items include breakfast options under $3, like the Sausage McMuffin, Sausage Biscuit, Sausage Burrito, Hash Browns, and a medium McCafé Premium Roast Coffee. For lunch or dinner, that same under-$3 value extends to the McChicken, McDouble, 4-Piece Chicken McNuggets, small fries, and a medium soft drink.

McDonald's (MCD) is also bringing the fan-favorite Meal Deal to breakfast. Customers can choose a sausage McMuffin or sausage biscuit, served with hash browns and a small McCafé Premium Roast Coffee, all for just $4.

At the $5 price point, the chain is offering a McChicken Meal Deal with 4-piece McNuggets, small fries, and a small soft drink.

Prices and participation levels can vary at some locations.

"As our customers' expectations evolve, we're making it easier for them to get the value they're looking for—on their terms. McValue offers more choice, more flexibility, and more ways to build a meal that fits their day and budget," highlighted McDonald's (MCD) USA Chief Marketing and Customer Experience Officer Alyssa Buetikofer.

McDonald's (MCD) has been leaning harder into discounts and meal deals as consumers remain sensitive to fast-food pricing. The company has already used value promotions to drive traffic, and the new McValue update is part of that broader strategy.

The McDonald's (MCD) pricing move could impact chains such as Wendy's (WEN), Taco Bell (YUM), Jack in the Box (JACK), Sonic, Whataburger, Culver's, Five Guys, Freddy’s Frozen Custard & Steakburgers, Hardee's, Carl's Jr., Checkers/Rally's, In-N-Out, Habit Burger (YUM), Smashburger, White Castle, Dairy Queen, Arby's, and Shake Shack (SHAK) to varying degrees.

Shares of McDonald's (MCD) were down 0.3% in premarket trading. The restaurant stock is down 2.0% over the last 52 weeks.

data.title: Sally Beauty names Adrianne Lee to CFO post
data.description:
• Sally Beauty (SBH) said that Adrianne Lee has been appointed senior VP and CFO, effective April 28, 2026. 
  • Ms. Lee succeeds Marlo Cormier, who will be leaving the company, effective April 11, 2026, to pursue other opportunities.
  • Ms. Lee brings significant relevant experience to the role, most recently serving as President and CFO at Bed Bath & Beyond.

data.title: Pharvaris reports Q4 results
data.description: • Pharvaris press release (PHVS): Q4 loss was €46.7 million, resulting in basic and diluted loss per share of €0.72.

data.title: Scotiabank gets approval for new buyback of up to 15M shares
data.description:
• Scotiabank (BNS) has received regulatory approval for a new normal course issuer bid to repurchase up to 15M shares, representing about 1.2% of its outstanding shares.
  • The purchases under the program are set to begin on April 7, 2026, and will run through April 6, 2027, unless completed earlier.
  • The move follows the bank’s previous buyback, under which it repurchased 20M shares at an average price of about C$90.47, deploying approximately C$1.81B.
  • The lender said that the new bid provides added flexibility for the bank to manage its capital position, offset options-exercise dilution, and generate shareholder value.

data.title: ESAB announces CFO transition
data.description:
• ESAB (ESAB) announced R. Brent Jones has been appointed CFO, effective early May 2026. 

  • Mr. Jones succeeds Kevin Johnson, who is leaving ESAB to pursue a CFO opportunity at a privately held company. Kevin will support ESAB in facilitating a smooth transition.

  • Mr. Jones brings over three decades of experience to ESAB (ESAB) and has served as CFO for several public companies, including Pall Corporation and, most recently, Avantor.
  • The company also reaffirms its 2026 total core sales, aEBITDA and aEPS guidance ranges, which were previously announced on February 2, 2026.

data.title: Lindsay GAAP EPS of $1.15 misses by $0.54, revenue of $157.7M misses by $13.69M
data.description:
• Lindsay press release (LNN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.15 misses by $0.54.
  • Net earnings were impacted by lower operating income and a higher effective tax rate, both of which were partially offset by an increase in other income.
  • Revenue of $157.7M (-15.7% Y/Y) misses by $13.69M.
  • Revenues for the second quarter of fiscal 2026 were $157.7 million, a decrease of $29.3 million, or 16 percent, compared to $187.1 million in the prior year. The decrease was primarily driven by lower revenues in both the irrigation and infrastructure segments compared to the prior year.
  • Shares -3.5% PM.

data.title: Sam's Club fee increase is expected to boost Walmart, Costco, and BJ's
data.description:
Walmart (WMT) is being sized up by analysts after announcing the first membership fee increase for Sam's Club since 2022. Starting on May 1, the retailer chain's club tier annual fee will rise to $60 from $50, while the fee for plus members will increase to $120 from $110. Of note, the $10 increase is the largest nominal fee increase for basic members in recent history.

Jefferies analyst Corey Tarlow said the firm views the increase as supporting continued reinvestment in value, including a higher Sam’s Cash annual cap for plus members to $750 from $500. 

"While modest near-term renewal headwinds are possible, we view the increase as structurally positive for Sam’s earnings power given high incremental margins on membership revenue. We assume WMT would not raise fees if renewal trends were at risk. More broadly, the action further normalizes higher fee levels across the category, supporting long-term revenue and profit assumptions for the club group," updated Tarlowe. That last part means BJ's Wholesale Club (BJ) and Costco (COST) could benefit as well.

Jefferies has a Buy rating on Walmart (WMT) and a price target of $145.

Shares of Walmart (WMT) are up 12.0% on a year-to-date basis and have topped broad market averages due in part to the defensive positioning of investors. The stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating on Wall Street, while Seeking Alpha analysts are more cautious.

data.title: Immunovant falls on late-stage trial setback for TED therapy
data.description:
Immunovant (IMVT) shares lost ~10% in the premarket on Thursday after the company, a subsidiary of Roivant Sciences (ROIV), announced a failure in a late-stage program for batoclimab, an experimental therapy for thyroid eye disease.

The New York-based biotech said that two Phase 3 trials evaluating batoclimab in adults with active, moderate-to-severe TED failed to meet their primary endpoint, ≥2mm proptosis responder rate at Week 24.

However, both studies indicated safety data consistent with previous findings, and there were no new safety signals, according to Immunovant (IMVT).

Given the setback, the company said that it will share an update on its development program for batoclimab after reviewing the plans with its South Korean partner, HanAll Biopharma.

In the meantime, Immunovant (IMVT) intends to focus on expediting the clinical advancement of IMVT-1402. Topline data from potentially registrational studies for the FcRn blocker against an autoimmune disorder called Graves’ disease are expected next year.
